name: influenza
description: Influenza is a contagious respiratory illness caused by influenza viruses. It can cause mild to severe illness and can lead to hospitalisation or death in vulnerable groups.
common_causes: Touching contaminated surfaces then touching face
general_care_tips: Rest and stay hydrated
Use over-the-counter fever reducers if needed (consult pharmacist)
Avoid contact with others to prevent spread
Wash hands frequently
Antiviral medications may help if started early — discuss with a doctor
when_to_seek_care: Seek care immediately if you have difficulty breathing, persistent chest pain, confusion, severe vomiting, or if symptoms improve then return with fever and worsening cough.

name: common cold
description: The common cold is a viral infection of the upper respiratory tract, most often caused by rhinoviruses. It is generally mild and self-limiting.
general_care_tips: Rest well
Stay hydrated with water, warm broths, or herbal teas
Saline nasal rinses may relieve congestion
Honey and lemon may soothe a sore throat
Symptoms typically resolve within 7–10 days
when_to_seek_care: See a doctor if symptoms worsen after 10 days, if you develop high fever, shortness of breath, ear pain, or if a child under 3 months develops a fever.

name: COVID-19
description: COVID-19 is an infectious disease caused by the SARS-CoV-2 coronavirus. It primarily affects the respiratory system but can affect multiple organ systems.
general_care_tips: Isolate to prevent spread
Rest and maintain hydration
Monitor oxygen levels if possible
Follow current health authority guidance on treatment and isolation
Most people recover without hospital care
when_to_seek_care: Seek emergency care if you experience difficulty breathing, persistent chest pain or pressure, confusion, or inability to stay awake.

name: asthma
description: Asthma is a chronic lung disease that inflames and narrows the airways, causing recurring episodes of wheezing, breathlessness, chest tightness, and coughing.
common_causes: Allergic triggers (pollen, dust mites, pet dander)
typical_symptoms: Coughing (especially at night or early morning)
general_care_tips: Identify and avoid personal triggers
Use prescribed inhalers as directed
Keep a symptom diary
Maintain a clean home environment to reduce allergens
Exercise regularly, but warm up properly
when_to_seek_care: Seek immediate care if breathing is very difficult, if a reliever inhaler has no effect, or if lips or fingernails turn blue.

name: pneumonia
description: Pneumonia is an infection that inflames the air sacs in one or both lungs, which may fill with fluid. It can be caused by bacteria, viruses, or fungi.
typical_symptoms: Chest pain when breathing or coughing
general_care_tips: Rest and hydration are essential
Follow prescribed antibiotic or antiviral treatment
Use a humidifier to ease breathing
Avoid smoking and secondhand smoke
when_to_seek_care: Seek immediate care for high fever (above 39°C/102°F), difficulty breathing, blue lips, or confusion, especially in the elderly and young children.

name: urinary tract infection (UTI)
description: A urinary tract infection (UTI) is an infection in any part of the urinary system — kidneys, ureters, bladder, and urethra. Most infections involve the lower urinary tract.
typical_symptoms: Frequent urination with little urine passed
Lower back or side pain (if kidneys involved)
general_care_tips: Drink plenty of water to flush bacteria
Urinate after sexual activity
Wipe front to back
Avoid irritating feminine products
Antibiotics are typically required — see a doctor
when_to_seek_care: See a doctor if symptoms appear, especially if fever, back pain, nausea, or vomiting develop (signs of kidney infection).
prevention: Avoid holding urine for long periods

name: migraine
description: Migraine is a neurological condition characterised by intense, debilitating headaches, often accompanied by nausea, vomiting, and sensitivity to light and sound.
typical_symptoms: Severe throbbing or pulsing headache (often one-sided)
general_care_tips: Rest in a quiet, dark room during an attack
Apply a cold or warm compress to the head
Stay hydrated
Keep a migraine diary to identify triggers
Over-the-counter pain relievers may help in mild cases
when_to_seek_care: Seek emergency care for a sudden, severe 'thunderclap' headache, headache with fever and stiff neck, headache after head injury, or new headache over age 50.
prevention: Prescription preventive medications may be discussed with a neurologist

name: gastroenteritis
description: Gastroenteritis is inflammation of the stomach and intestines, typically resulting from bacterial toxins, viral infection, or parasites.
general_care_tips: Prioritise rehydration — oral rehydration salts (ORS) are highly effective
Eat bland foods once vomiting subsides (rice, toast, bananas)
Avoid dairy, caffeine, and fatty foods temporarily
Wash hands thoroughly to avoid spreading infection
when_to_seek_care: Seek care if diarrhoea persists beyond 3 days, if there is blood in stool, severe abdominal pain, signs of dehydration (dry mouth, no urination, dizziness), or in very young children and the elderly.

name: irritable bowel syndrome (IBS)
description: IBS is a common functional gastrointestinal disorder characterised by abdominal pain and changes in bowel habits without detectable structural damage.
typical_symptoms: Abdominal pain or cramping (often relieved by bowel movement)
general_care_tips: Follow a low-FODMAP diet (under dietitian guidance)
Manage stress through exercise, yoga, or mindfulness
Stay hydrated and eat regular meals
Keep a food and symptom diary
Avoid carbonated drinks and caffeine
when_to_seek_care: See a doctor if you notice blood in stool, unexplained weight loss, onset after age 50, or if symptoms are severely impacting daily life.

name: eczema
description: Eczema is a chronic skin condition characterised by itchy, inflamed, and cracked skin. It often begins in childhood and may persist into adulthood.
typical_symptoms: Small, raised bumps that may weep fluid
general_care_tips: Moisturise skin daily with fragrance-free products
Use gentle, non-irritating soaps
Avoid scratching; keep fingernails short
Wear soft, breathable fabrics (cotton)
Identify and avoid personal triggers
when_to_seek_care: See a doctor or dermatologist if itching severely disrupts sleep or daily activities, if the rash becomes infected (yellow crusting, warmth, swelling), or if over-the-counter treatments are not effective.
prevention: Use mild, fragrance-free skin care products

name: osteoarthritis
description: Osteoarthritis is the most common form of arthritis, involving the breakdown of cartilage in joints. It most commonly affects the knees, hips, and hands.
typical_symptoms: Joint pain during or after movement
Morning stiffness lasting less than 30 minutes
general_care_tips: Maintain a healthy weight to reduce joint stress
Regular low-impact exercise (swimming, cycling, walking)
Physical therapy and strengthening exercises
Apply heat or cold packs to affected joints
Assistive devices may reduce strain
when_to_seek_care: Consult a doctor if joint pain persists, limits your daily activities, or if you notice significant swelling, redness, or warmth around a joint.

name: rheumatoid arthritis
description: Rheumatoid arthritis is a chronic autoimmune disease where the immune system attacks joint linings, causing inflammation, pain, and potentially joint deformity.
typical_symptoms: Joint stiffness (worst in the morning or after inactivity)
general_care_tips: Follow prescribed disease-modifying medications consistently
Balance rest and activity — avoid overexertion during flares
Physiotherapy to maintain joint function
Warm baths or compresses can ease morning stiffness
Omega-3 rich diet may support inflammation management
when_to_seek_care: See a doctor promptly if multiple joints become swollen and tender, especially with morning stiffness lasting over an hour. Early treatment prevents joint damage.

name: diabetes mellitus
description: Diabetes mellitus is a group of metabolic diseases characterised by high blood sugar levels due to insulin deficiency or resistance.
common_causes: Type 1: Autoimmune destruction of insulin-producing beta cells
Type 2: Insulin resistance combined with impaired insulin secretion, driven by obesity, inactivity, and genetics
general_care_tips: Monitor blood glucose levels regularly
Follow a balanced diet low in refined sugars and processed foods
Regular physical activity improves insulin sensitivity
Take medications or insulin as prescribed
Attend regular check-ups for eye, kidney, and foot health
when_to_seek_care: Seek emergency care for symptoms of diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA): excessive thirst, fruity-smelling breath, vomiting, confusion. Regular monitoring is essential to prevent complications.
prevention: Balanced diet low in refined carbohydrates

name: hypothyroidism
description: Hypothyroidism is a condition where the thyroid gland does not produce enough thyroid hormone, slowing down many of the body's functions.
general_care_tips: Take levothyroxine medication consistently as prescribed
Avoid taking medication with calcium, iron, or antacids
Regular thyroid function blood tests
A balanced diet with adequate iodine supports thyroid health
when_to_seek_care: See a doctor if you experience persistent fatigue, unexplained weight gain, cold intolerance, or depression. A blood test (TSH) confirms diagnosis.
prevention: Regular thyroid screening in at-risk groups (women over 60, family history)

name: hyperthyroidism
description: Hyperthyroidism occurs when the thyroid gland produces too much thyroid hormone, accelerating the body's metabolism.
general_care_tips: Follow prescribed medications (antithyroid drugs) consistently
Avoid excessive iodine in diet (e.g., seaweed)
Wear sunglasses if experiencing eye issues (Graves' ophthalmopathy)
Regular monitoring of thyroid hormone levels
when_to_seek_care: Seek urgent care for rapid heart rate, chest pain, or shortness of breath. A thyroid storm is a medical emergency — seek immediate help.
prevention: No guaranteed prevention; early diagnosis reduces complications

name: anaemia
description: Anaemia is a condition where the blood lacks sufficient healthy red blood cells to carry adequate oxygen to body tissues.
general_care_tips: Eat iron-rich foods (red meat, beans, spinach, fortified cereals)
Pair iron foods with vitamin C to boost absorption
Take prescribed iron or B12 supplements
Avoid tea/coffee with meals as they inhibit iron absorption
when_to_seek_care: See a doctor if you experience persistent fatigue, paleness, shortness of breath, or rapid heartbeat. A blood test (CBC) confirms the diagnosis and type of anaemia.

name: polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS)
description: PCOS is a hormonal disorder common among women of reproductive age, characterised by irregular periods, excess androgen, and polycystic ovaries.
typical_symptoms: Darkening of skin in skin folds
general_care_tips: Maintain a healthy weight — even 5–10% weight loss can improve symptoms
Regular exercise helps regulate insulin levels
Follow a low-glycaemic index (GI) diet
Manage stress levels
Follow prescribed hormone therapy if advised by a doctor
when_to_seek_care: See a gynaecologist or endocrinologist if you have irregular periods, signs of excess androgens, or difficulty conceiving.
prevention: Healthy lifestyle to manage insulin resistance

name: sleep apnoea
description: Sleep apnoea is a serious sleep disorder where breathing repeatedly stops and starts during sleep, reducing oxygen delivery to the body.
general_care_tips: Lose weight if overweight
Avoid alcohol and sedatives before bedtime
Sleep on your side rather than back
Follow CPAP therapy as prescribed
Quit smoking
when_to_seek_care: See a doctor or sleep specialist if you are frequently very tired despite adequate sleep, snore loudly, or if your partner reports pauses in your breathing.
specialist_type: Sleep Medicine Specialist / ENT Surgeon / Pulmonologist

name: depression
description: Depression is a common mental health disorder characterised by persistent sadness, loss of interest, and a range of physical and cognitive symptoms that interfere with daily life.
typical_symptoms: Loss of interest in activities once enjoyed
general_care_tips: Speak with a mental health professional — therapy is highly effective
Regular physical exercise has proven antidepressant effects
Maintain a regular sleep schedule
Stay socially connected
Avoid alcohol
Follow prescribed antidepressant medication as directed
when_to_seek_care: Seek immediate care if you experience thoughts of self-harm or suicide. Contact a crisis helpline or emergency services. See a doctor promptly if symptoms persist for more than two weeks.

name: heart failure
description: Heart failure is a chronic condition where the heart is unable to pump blood effectively to meet the body's needs. It is a serious but manageable condition.
typical_symptoms: Shortness of breath during activity or when lying flat
Swelling in legs, ankles, and feet
general_care_tips: Follow a low-sodium diet to reduce fluid retention
Weigh yourself daily and report sudden weight gain to your doctor
Take all medications as prescribed (do not stop without medical advice)
Limit fluid intake as directed
Moderate activity as tolerated under medical guidance
when_to_seek_care: Seek emergency care for sudden shortness of breath, chest pain, rapid weight gain (more than 1 kg in a day or 2 kg in a week), or worsening leg swelling.

name: kidney stones
description: Kidney stones are hard deposits made of minerals and salts that form inside the kidneys and can cause severe pain when passing through the urinary tract.
common_causes: Certain supplements (excess calcium or vitamin D)
typical_symptoms: Severe pain in the back, side, or lower abdomen
Blood in urine (pink, red, or brown)
general_care_tips: Drink 2–3 litres of water daily
Reduce sodium and animal protein intake
Limit oxalate-rich foods if prone to calcium oxalate stones
Lemon juice (citrate) may help prevent some stone types
when_to_seek_care: Seek urgent care for severe pain that you cannot manage, fever and chills with urinary symptoms, or inability to pass urine.
prevention: Balanced diet low in sodium and animal protein

name: gallstones
description: Gallstones are hardened deposits in the gallbladder that form when substances in bile (such as cholesterol) crystallise.
typical_symptoms: Sudden severe pain in upper right abdomen
Pain radiating to right shoulder or back
general_care_tips: Eat a low-fat, high-fibre diet
Maintain a healthy weight
Avoid rapid weight loss
Exercise regularly
Follow prescribed medications or prepare for surgery as advised
when_to_seek_care: Seek emergency care for severe abdominal pain, high fever with chills, jaundice, or if pain is persistent and spreading.

name: tuberculosis
description: Tuberculosis is a potentially serious infectious disease caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis, primarily affecting the lungs but capable of affecting other organs.
common_causes: Airborne transmission via coughing, sneezing, or speaking
Close, prolonged contact with an infected individual
typical_symptoms: Persistent cough lasting more than 3 weeks
general_care_tips: Complete the full course of antibiotic treatment (typically 6 months) — stopping early causes drug resistance
Cover your mouth when coughing or sneezing
Ensure good ventilation in living spaces
Inform close contacts so they can be tested
when_to_seek_care: See a doctor promptly if you have a cough lasting more than 3 weeks, cough blood, or have unexplained weight loss with night sweats.
prevention: HIV testing (TB and HIV are closely linked)

name: psoriasis
description: Psoriasis is a chronic autoimmune skin disease that speeds up the life cycle of skin cells, causing cells to build up rapidly on the surface.
common_causes: Triggers: stress, infections, medications, skin injury
typical_symptoms: Red patches of skin covered with thick, silvery scales
Dry, cracked skin that may bleed or itch
general_care_tips: Moisturise skin regularly
Keep baths lukewarm and short
Use medicated shampoos for scalp psoriasis
Expose skin to small amounts of natural sunlight carefully
Manage stress levels
Avoid known triggers
when_to_seek_care: Consult a dermatologist if symptoms are severe, if the rash is widespread, if it affects daily life, or if you develop swollen and painful joints.

name: hepatitis B
description: Hepatitis B is a serious liver infection caused by the hepatitis B virus (HBV). It can become a chronic condition leading to liver failure, cirrhosis, or liver cancer.
common_causes: Unprotected sexual contact with an infected person
general_care_tips: Follow prescribed antiviral medications for chronic hepatitis B
Avoid alcohol completely — it accelerates liver damage
Eat a liver-friendly diet (low fat, low salt, high fibre)
Get vaccinated contacts and family members
Attend regular liver function monitoring
when_to_seek_care: See a doctor if you have jaundice, abdominal swelling, severe fatigue, or any exposure risk. A blood test confirms infection.

name: allergic rhinitis
description: Allergic rhinitis is an allergic response to specific allergens like pollen, dust mites, or pet dander that causes cold-like symptoms.
general_care_tips: Monitor pollen counts and limit outdoor exposure on high-pollen days
Keep windows closed during pollen season
Use HEPA air purifiers
Shower after being outdoors
Antihistamines and nasal corticosteroid sprays are effective — consult a pharmacist or doctor
when_to_seek_care: See a doctor or allergist if symptoms severely affect quality of life, don't respond to over-the-counter treatments, or are accompanied by asthma symptoms.
prevention: Allergen immunotherapy (allergy shots) for long-term relief
